item.content:encoded: Congrats on the +100! Mine's the last upvote. I guess the skip got there before but someone else downvoted it. The mental image is just too good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is kind of interesting, but this is just too short imo. There's just not much going on here. This is gonna be a -1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a weird little thing that does its job and does it well. Great work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Drakerunner really hammered all my complaints on it. Upvote when amended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like this, but there are a few points that really hammered down on my suspension of disbelief.

When did the disease manifest? It seems strange that hundreds of thousands of individuals a year contracted disease that moves them toward the single location, and the foundation has been able to keep this under wraps in perpetua. It should be pretty awful for transients living on coastal cities.

And no one in Nebraska seems to be bothered by the yearly hobofront moving in? What about the individuals who did not have the nutrition to make the journey, and died en route? Shouldn't Nebraska have a significantly higher transient population, since there is free airfare?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. This is fantastic. You've got a future here, author.
  2. Thank you for kicking 2371 off its throne as the highest-rated of the month so far. As much as I want to be recognized, I don't want to be recognized for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing Nebraska is where the Big Rock Candy Mountain used to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I go to the end of the article and thought to myself "This had better be listed as 'Drifters' on the main series page". +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for the compliments and the note on the MTF name; I've fixed that nitpick. And I do plan on writing more! I've got a thread up in the Draft forums now for my second :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fly free, o hobos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well then. What a pleasant surprise. A nice, sweet short article with a punch… and it's your first!

My only nitpick is that MTF nicknames are usually place in single apostrophes and not brackets. It should be 'Balloon Men', not (Balloon Men). Other than that, solid, solid article.

Great job author! I hope you keep writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't dislike it, but nor does it really do anything for me. No vote

Actually on second reading, I enjoyed it enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, well formatted, and to the point.

I like it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and interesting. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's weird. What's going on? Nobody knows, not even the know-it-alls at the Foundation.

I like it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tried a rough latitude/longitude search and it put a pin on Windy Hill, NE. I like that.

I'm a big fan of short SCP's with little to no backstory, as long as it feels like the author knows more of the story than they're telling us. This one definitely feels like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It just doesn't 'come together' for me. Some SCPs can stand on their own as just some shit that happens, but "transients get bronchitis and float to Nebraska" isn't one of them. I'll have to -1.

Otherwise, though, this is very solid work, and I'm confident that you'll produce good material in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great work, it's short but executed nicely. Plus, it's such a weird concept I can't help but like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I guess it is charming to see the Foundation confront a disease SCP… like a disease.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bravo. A new one to point to for proof that there is no first SCP curse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They keep floating around until the disease works its way through their system. That's why the site is built nearby, so they can stash infectees without them bouncing against the walls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is fantastic, especially for a first scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are too many bits of phrasing that sound too weird for me to upvote this.

"deemed completely eliminated"; "including cough"; "begin to slowly become more buoyant"; "without attendant decrease in mass or increase in volume"; the sentence including "significant deviations"

These (and other sentences) could be phrased more precisely, clinically and grammatically. In particular, I feel that there is a lack of accurate scientific terminology throughout the article, which prevents me from believing that a(n in-universe) scientist could have written this.

edit: I was pretty drunk when I wrote this so disregard it all

item.title: Just wondering.
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling that "Item Number" is made to be "Item #" but who knows, it might just be a diff style of writing the SCP.

Yeah, I have checked a bunch of SCPs and I am 99.9% sure that you need to add Item #.

Good job! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me wants there to be more to this, but if nothing else, you've allowed me to share in the Foundation's own sense of "What the actual fuck is going on here?" and that's pretty powerful.

Hobolloons. Now I've seen everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the fascinating mental image of hobos getting Violet Bureaugard disease, I must congratulate you on actually properly contextualizing a description in quotes (even if it stretches my suspension of disbelief to have multiple people say the exact same thing).

I must ask though, what happens upon reaching the Convergence Point? Does the disease go away or do the diseased just keep floating around?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A: It's a 300% higher infection rate. So it's a ~10% rate among most people, but a ~40% rate (if I remember my statistics properly) among the target group.
B: I did try to add a little extra implication that there was something underground at the location, but it sort of devolved into weird headcannons when I was thinking about it, so I decided to only hint rather than force the ideas on others.
And you did look at this in the forum; thanks again for that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. a 300% infection rate is impossible. I misread, disregard.
B. I think I remember reading this in chat or on the forum several weeks ago. I liked it then, but this feels a little too short. You have a neat idea here, but it doesn't really have that extra unique factor to make it a great scip. Neutral vote for now. Upon reread: fuck it, I like this article. It's short, and sort of basic, but it does what it does really well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a pretty damn good, short and simple "thing what is weird" skip, IMO. Take an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First SCP; do with it what you will. Thanks to Dr. Solo for looking at this in the forums.
